steps.title: Identify the clause boundary
steps.content: Before the blank: "a complex network of radiating veins that provide structural support" — a complete independent clause. After: "... in thickness from the center to the edges, these veins allow..." — another independent clause with its own subject ("these veins").

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Two ICs require a period (or semicolon). The participle "Decreasing in thickness..." then opens a participial phrase modifying "these veins."

Choice A's period creates that needed sentence break.

B is a comma splice. C's "while decreasing" jams two ICs together with only a comma. D drops punctuation entirely.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
